title: teams — helper functions
description: Ergonomic helpers for accessing the active team from the request context, resolving polymorphic members, checking eligibility, computing capacity + waitlist availability. All helpers are thin wrappers around bound services — never business logic.

helpers.purpose: Returns the resolved Team for the current request (set by team.resolve_active middleware). Null when no team context.
helpers.example:
$team = active_team();
if ($team === null) throw new NoActiveTeamException();

helpers.purpose: Returns the resolved Team.id or null. Convenience for creators that need the ID without loading the model.

Given a polymorphic (member_type, member_id) pair, returns the resolved Eloquent model (Athlete / Coach / Staff / User) or null. Consults the polymorphism registry from config('teams.polymorphism.member_type_registry').
Returns true when the team has capacity for `delta` more active members (respecting capacity + capacity_waitlist). Skipped when team_capacity_gating entitlement is off (always true).
Check whether an athlete born on the given DOB fits within team.min_age/max_age. Returns true when the check is skipped (missing bounds, missing DOB).
Check whether a gender matches team.gender_category. `any` accepts everything. Missing gender = skipped (returns true).
Cached active-roster count for a team (WHERE status='active' AND deleted_at IS NULL). Cheap read for capacity + eligibility gating.
True when team.min_age < 18 OR max_age < 18 (minors on the roster). Consulted by the child-safety notification chain.

Atomic conversion of a trial to a team member inside a DB transaction. Refuses on uniqueness violation or capacity exceeded (rolls back). Fires TrialConvertedToMember + TeamMemberJoined in the afterCommit hook.

notes: Helpers deliberately delegate — testing swaps the bound service to a stub, and every helper follows.
No helper directly touches the DB — everything routes through repositories or services. Keeps the boundary clean.
resolve_team_member is the ONLY helper that reaches into the polymorphism registry. Every consumer of TeamMember.member goes through it — never through a bespoke switch statement.
